steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that snowball sampling yielded far more participants with the desired characteristics than chance would predict. The blank must explain why recruiting from seeds' social networks produced that result.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the characteristics that made the seeds desirable for inclusion in the proposed study may be unknown to some members of the seeds social networks.: if network members don't know what traits the seeds have, they can't selectively recruit — this would reduce, not explain, the above-chance success. ✗
  • Choice B — most seeds' social networks include some people who do not know one another and who share few characteristics.: networks with few shared characteristics would make above-chance success harder to achieve, not easier. ✗
  • Choice C — the seeds social networks tend to be somewhat smaller than the networks of people who do not live in rural areas.: network size does not explain why a high proportion of recruits had the desired characteristics. ✗
  • Choice D — the seeds' social networks include a high proportion of people who share characteristics with the seeds.: if the seeds' connections resemble the seeds, recruiting from those networks naturally yields participants with the desired traits — directly explaining the above-chance success. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
